title: Active Management and Performance Measurement: If an investor has an annual return of 0.15 and...
summary: Calculate the Sharpe ratio from portfolio excess return over the risk-free rate divided by standard deviation.
prompt: If an investor has an annual return of 15% and a risk-free rate of 5%, what is the investor's Sharpe ratio if the standard deviation of the portfolio return is 20%?
solution:
Step 1: Identify Variables
Return of portfolio (rp) = 15%, Risk-free rate (rf) = 5%, Standard deviation (σ) = 20%

Step 2: Use Sharpe Ratio Formula
Sharpe Ratio = (rp - rf) / σ
Sharpe Ratio = (0.15 - 0.05) / 0.20

Step 3: Calculate Sharpe Ratio
Sharpe Ratio = 0.10 / 0.20 = 0.5. Hence the Sharpe ratio is 0.5.
